title: Setting to only show downloaded chapters
author: ItsGlassPlus1
body:
### Describe your suggested feature

In the context of using suwayomi as a downloader and mihon as the reader, it'd be useful to have an option to only show chapters that are downloaded within suwayomi when using the extension. 

As it exists right now, there's no way to tell which chapters are downloaded and available from the server vs non downloaded without manually checking

title: Tracking to Suwayomi for non-Suwayomi source titles?
author: dougNetD
body:
### Describe your suggested feature

The same way you can add tracking to suwayomi on a title that **_IS_** sourced from suwayomi; could you track to suwayomi for non-suwayomi source titles on the mihon client if the same/similar title is found on the suwayomi server?
I thought it could be the same way AL, MAL tracking works in Mihon etc; by finding an appropriate title to select, but in this case from the suwayomi server. I can see how this might not be possible even with my limited understanding of these apps, since the library titles are tied to their source extensions and if suwayomi extension isn't in use for that title, then the suwayomi extension can't affect the tracking menu for that title? In that case suwayomi tracking support for all sources generally would have to be developed by the mihon app?

The use case i thought was with multiple client devices, if you didn't want some mihon client sourcing from your suwayomi server for whatever reason, but still wanted to track to suwayomi.

### Other details

_No response_

title: New chapters automatically marked as read when using Suwayomi Extension
author: pluusla
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Use Suwayomi as the primary source/server for mangas, and connect Mihon using the Suwayomi extension.
2. Ensure Suwayomi is also active as a tracker within Mihon to handle chapter sync.
3. In the extension settings, enable both Tracker: Delete downloaded files from suwayomi when marking chapters as read and Fetch Data from Source.
4. Let the Suwayomi server download a new chapter. (Important: The new chapter correctly shows as "unread" on the Suwayomi server side).
6. Refresh the library or the specific manga in Mihon.
7. Suwayomi chapter ist unread, in mihon the chapter is read

### Expected behavior

The newly downloaded chapter should appear in Mihon and be marked as "unread", exactly matching the current unread status on the Suwayomi server.

### Actual behavior

The new chapter appears in Mihon but is instantly and incorrectly marked as "read". I want to emphasize that on the Suwayomi server itself, the chapter is correctly listed as unread before and after the refresh. Because Suwayomi itself acts as the tracker in this setup to handle the deletion logic, this false local "read" state in Mihon directly disrupts the sync loop between the client and the server.

### App version

0.19.4

### Android version

16

### Other details

As Suwayomi is my self-hosted source, I heavily rely on the automatic deletion feature via the Suwayomi tracker to manage my server storage efficiently. Turning it off isn't a viable workaround.

It seems there is a conflict between the "Fetch Data from Source" option and the Suwayomi tracker's deletion logic. When the old chapters are physically deleted from the server, it might cause an index shift in the API response, leading Mihon to mistakenly assign the "read" flag to the new, unread chapters upon refresh.

Of course, it is also entirely possible that my setup is flawed or that I am completely misunderstanding how these specific settings are supposed to interact with each other. Any clarification or help to point me in the right direction would be highly appreciated!

(Note: Suwayomi Server Version: v2.1.1867, Suwayomi Extension Version: 1.4.25)

### Acknowledgements

- [x] I have searched the existing issues and this is a new ticket, **NOT** a duplicate or related to another open or closed issue.
- [x] I have written a short but informative title.
- [x] I have updated the app to the latest version.

title: Allow adding custom HTTP headers in settings
author: FlorianFreudiger
body:
### Describe your suggested feature

Similar to #11, my Suwayomi instance is behind an authentication proxy.

I do know that using the cookie-based login method allows one to authenticate using the built-in webview and keep the updated cookies. However this isn't ideal if the proxy only allows temporary access via cookie (e.g. Cloudflare) or if the proxy doesn't have any login page at all (e.g. custom auth via NGINX).

So custom HTTP headers would allow for greater flexibility when working with proxies, instead of only being restricted to make it work using the existing basic-/cookie-auth mechanics.

I know this might be a niche use case but I could work on adding the setting myself if this sounds reasonable.

### Other details

Auth examples that would be possible using this:
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Reference/Headers/Proxy-Authorization
(Note the header is for "Proxy-Authorization", not "Authorization")

https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/access-controls/service-credentials/service-tokens/#initial-request

### Acknowledgements

title: Missing a few `trimEnd('/')`
author: altaccosc
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Set URL in settings with a trailing `/`
2. Try to load a chapter

### Expected behavior

It should not error and work properly instead.

### Actual behavior

```
URL: http://my-server:4567//api/v1/manga/237/chapter/72/page/0

Bad Message 400
reason: Ambiguous URI empty segment
```

### App version

0.15.3

### Android version

12

### Other details

The cause is the trailing slash. If removed, it works.

I checked the code and in these places it seems like the trailing slash removal is not present yet:
- https://github.com/Suwayomi/tachiyomi-extension/blob/38b5eb035e0e6c6a88f64421b140d6af8b318231/src/all/tachidesk/src/eu/kanade/tachiyomi/extension/all/tachidesk/Tachidesk.kt#L229
- https://github.com/Suwayomi/tachiyomi-extension/blob/38b5eb035e0e6c6a88f64421b140d6af8b318231/src/all/tachidesk/src/eu/kanade/tachiyomi/extension/all/tachidesk/Tachidesk.kt#L661

title: Add fallback / settings to load chapter list from suwayomi directly instead triggering the chapter refresh online on suwayomi side
author: krakerz
body:
### Describe your suggested feature

If possible please add a fallback / settings so the extension read the chapter from the suwayomi library first, so it won't trigger the "refresh chapter" on suwayomi side (let just make suwayomi the ones that refresh the chapter list / update by it's own schedule)

this is for the case like this :
you already downloaded completed manga A in source A (in suwayomi)
then either the source A is gone or the manga A is deleted by the source
after that deletion, you want to add the manga A into mihon / tachiyomi

current result is, you can add it but with 0 chapter listed, because currently it's (i think its trigger online fetch chapter on suwayomi side) return will be error / null (although you already downloaded the complete manga A in suwayomi)

title: Ability to add to library and trigger downloads from inside mihon
author: SyntheticGoop
body:
### Describe your suggested feature

The way I use suwayomi right now is as a caching proxy to a bigger library than I can keep on my phone, and also as an archiver.

It is slightly inconvenient that the steps I have to take to set up that workflow is to
1. Go to suwayomi directly and add + tag the manga
2. Trigger the download on suwayomi
3. Go into mihon
4. Add to library

Will it be possible for the following workflowsto exist
1. Search in mihon directly, proxying the search through the extensions in suwayomi.
2. Add and tag inside mihon.
3. When multiple chapters are read/downloaded in mihon and proxied through suwayomi, add to library in suwayomi and start downloading all.

### Other details

_No response_

title: Syncing Problem with mihon
author: Black-Speedy
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Insert Suwayomi server info
2. Nothing happens

### Expected behavior

I excpect it to sync 

### Actual behavior

Nothing happens

### App version

1.14.6

### Android version

14

### Other details

I am want to use Suwayomi as a central server but it seems hard when I want the ability to download chapters to a local device such as my phone but it won't sync or do anything that shows me that it has connected. I have verified that I can connect to the Suwayomi server in a browser

title: Extension Tracker URL error (Separate from server url defined in settings)
author: Enhancifire
body:
### Steps to reproduce

Suwayomi Extension is set to ip address 100.x.x.x in Mihon. Trackers are set up to sync Suwayomi server with Mihon.
User clicks on tracking tab in manga details page.

### Expected behavior

Popup should contain the correct number of chapters and update accordingly.

### Actual behavior

Tracker is showing "Suwayomi error: Failed to connect to /192.168.1.6:4567".
Updating the manga through the server works. It's only the tracker that's not working.

title: add webview for custom auth
author: Kickunio
body:
### Describe your suggested feature

As the title says - When someone has a custom auth system (authelia, authentik, oauth2proxy, etc.) it shows error 401. By adding the webview mode to the extensions you could login using webview and then use the extension as intended.
